meta.title: Academic English Grammar Assessment
meta.subtitle: Advanced Proficiency Level (B2-C1) for University Preparatory Programs

sections.label: Part I: Advanced Sentence Structure and Tense Usage
Part III: Contextual Grammar and Cohesion

passages.text: The implementation of renewable energy systems (36) ______ a subject of intense debate among policymakers for decades. Despite the significant initial costs, many argue that the long-term benefits (37) ______ the drawbacks. If the government (38) ______ more proactively in the early 2000s, the current energy crisis might have been averted. Not only (39) ______ these systems reduce carbon emissions, but they also foster energy independence. Consequently, researchers (40) ______ new methods to enhance battery storage efficiency for several years now. (41) ______, the transition remains slow due to geopolitical constraints. It is essential that the international community (42) ______ together to standardize regulations. Had we known the extent of global warming earlier, we (43) ______ different choices. Many environmentalists suggest (44) ______ subsidies for fossil fuels immediately. By the time the next decade ends, it is hoped that green energy (45) ______ the primary source of power. (46) ______ the high cost of installation, many households are switching to solar panels. The technology is (47) ______ advanced that even cloudy regions can benefit. (48) ______ some critics claim the technology is unreliable, data shows otherwise. It is high time we (49) ______ our infrastructure to accommodate these changes. We look forward to (50) ______ a cleaner, more sustainable planet.

questions.question: Rarely ______ such a complex socio-economic phenomenon without conducting extensive longitudinal studies.

explanation.tactic: Cümle 'Rarely' (Nadir) gibi olumsuz/sınırlayıcı bir zarf ile başladığı için devrik yapı (inversion) gerektirir. Soru formu gibi yardımcı fiil (do/does/did) öznenin önüne gelmelidir.
explanation.trap: A şıkkı düz cümle yapısıdır; cümlenin başındaki 'Rarely' bu yapıyı bozar.

questions.question: By the time the delegation arrives next month, the committee ______ the final draft of the proposal.

explanation.tactic: 'By the time' + Present Simple (arrives) yapısı, ana cümlede 'Future Perfect' (will have done) kullanımını işaret eder. Belirli bir gelecekteki zamana kadar tamamlanmış eylemleri anlatır.
explanation.grammar: Future Perfect Tense (By the time sequence)
explanation.trap: C şıkkı Present Perfect'tir; ancak 'next month' geleceğe işaret ettiği için Future Perfect şarttır.

questions.question: The professor suggested that the research paper ______ before the end of the semester to allow for peer review.

explanation.tactic: 'Suggest that' yapısından sonra fiil yalın halde (subjunctive mood) kullanılır. Kağıdın teslim edilmesi 'edilgen' (passive) bir durum olduğu için 'be submitted' yapısı seçilir.
explanation.trap: A şıkkı (is submitted) subjunctive kuralına uymaz; suggest'ten sonra 'should' veya 'yalın fiil' gelir.

questions.question: Had the researchers known about the contamination in the samples, they ______ the experiment much sooner.

explanation.tactic: Cümle 'Had + Subject + V3' (If they had known) ile başlamıştır; yani Type 3 Conditional yapısındayız. Temel cümle 'would have V3' yapısında olmalıdır.
explanation.trap: A şıkkı Type 2 sonucudur; ancak 'had known' geçmişe yönelik bir varsayımdır.

questions.question: The theory of relativity, ______ by Albert Einstein, revolutionized our understanding of space and time.

explanation.tactic: İki virgül arası bir niteler sıfat (relative clause) kısaltmasıdır. 'Relativity' teorisi 'teklif edilen' (pasif) olduğu için 'which was proposed' yerine sadece V3 (proposed) kullanılır.
explanation.trap: A şıkkı aktiftir (öneren teori olurdu); C şıkkı 'that was' virgül sonrası (non-defining) kullanılmaz.

questions.question: The university requires that every student ______ an ethics seminar during their first year of study.
The more stringent the environmental regulations become, ______ it is for small businesses to comply without subsidies.
The CEO, ______ leadership style was often criticized, managed to double the company's revenue in two years.
No sooner ______ the podium than the audience erupted into a standing ovation.
I would rather you ______ the confidential documents via an encrypted server instead of standard email.
The witness denied ______ the defendant near the crime scene on the night of the incident.
Provided that the data ______ accurately, the software should generate the report within minutes.
Despite ______ for several hours, the jury was unable to reach a unanimous verdict.
It is essential that the patient ______ absolute bed rest for at least forty-eight hours.
The results of the survey were ______ disappointing that the marketing strategy had to be completely overhauled.
If the architect ______ more attention to the structural integrity, the building wouldn't have collapsed during the earthquake.
Little ______ that the small start-up would eventually become a global tech giant.
The scientist found it difficult to explain the phenomenon, ______ she had spent years studying it.
Not until the late 19th century ______ to gain widespread recognition as a legitimate field of science.
The committee members are looking forward to ______ the final decision at the upcoming board meeting.
The newly (A) developed vaccine, (B) what was tested (C) extensively, proved to be (D) highly effective against the virus.
If the manager (A) would have listened to the (B) employees' concerns, the (C) strike might have (D) been avoided.
The (A) number of students (B) enrolling in the computer science (C) program have (D) increased significantly this year.
(A) Despite it was (B) raining heavily, the (C) outdoor concert continued (D) as scheduled.
The (A) information (B) provided in the brochure (C) were found to be (D) inaccurate by the investigators.
Neither the (A) lead researcher nor his (B) assistants (C) was aware of the (D) discrepancy in the data.
I suggest (A) to consult a specialist (B) before making a (C) final decision on (D) the surgery.
The city (A) is known for (B) its beautiful parks, (C) which many tourists (D) visit them every year.
She (A) has been (B) working as a consultant (C) since three years (D) now.
Hardly (A) the speaker (B) had begun (C) when he was (D) interrupted by a heckler.
The (A) most unique (B) feature of this (C) software is its ability to (D) translate languages in real-time.
The (A) reason why he (B) failed the exam was (C) because he did not (D) study hard enough.
Each of the (A) candidates (B) were given ten minutes (C) to present (D) their proposals.
The (A) criteria for the scholarship (B) is very strict, (C) making it difficult (D) for most students to qualify.
Not only (A) did the storm (B) damage the roof, but it (C) also flooded the (D) basement either.
